articles.title: Meningitis Vaccines for Teenagers in Kent
articles.summary: In Kent, England, some Year 11 students at four schools are getting meningitis B vaccines to keep them safe. This is because there was an outbreak of meningitis, a serious illness, earlier this month. Two people died from it, and others got very sick. Health officials are giving vaccines to 15 and 16-year-olds as a precaution to protect them for a longer time. No new cases have been reported for two days, which is good news. Many people have already received vaccines and antibiotics to stop the spread. The outbreak mostly affected young people who went to a club or live at a university. Students are worried, so they are getting vaccinated to stay healthy.

articles.title: TV Star Mel Schilling Passes Away After Illness
articles.summary: Mel Schilling, a famous coach on the TV show 'Married at First Sight,' has died at age 54. Her husband shared the sad news on Instagram. Mel was diagnosed with colon cancer in December 2023, and recently, the cancer spread to her brain. She passed away peacefully, surrounded by love from her family. Mel was known for helping people find love on the show since 2016. Her husband said she was a brave and kind person who never complained, even during tough times. He encouraged everyone to live life fully and love others well in her memory. Channel 4, the TV network, also expressed sadness and praised Mel for her joy and positivity.

articles.title: New Homes Get Solar Panels and Heat Pumps
articles.summary: In England, the government has made a new rule for building homes. Starting in 2028, all new houses must have solar panels on their roofs and use heat pumps instead of gas for heating. Solar panels use sunlight to make electricity, and heat pumps are machines that keep homes warm without burning fossil fuels. This change is part of a plan called the Future Homes Standard to make homes better for the environment. It might cost more to build these homes, but it can save money on energy bills over time. Some people like this idea because it helps fight climate change, but builders are worried about the extra cost. The government says it's important to use clean power to stay safe and not rely on other countries for energy.

articles.title: Vets Must Show Prices and Cap Prescription Fees to Help Pet Owners
articles.summary: In the UK, a watchdog called the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) has made new rules for vets to help pet owners. Vets must now publish their price lists so people can see how much services cost. Also, fees for written prescriptions, which let pet owners buy medicine elsewhere, will be capped at £21 for the first medicine and £12.50 for more. This is because prices have been rising fast, and many pet owners didn't know they could save money by shopping around. The CMA found that over 70% of people buy medicine from their vet, but they could save £200 a year or more by looking online. Vets will also have to tell owners if they are part of a large group, so it's clearer who owns the practice. These changes aim to make it easier for pet owners to choose the best care for their pets without surprises. The rules will start later this year, and the British Veterinary Association says they will help people make better choices.

articles.title: Senate to Vote on New Homeland Security Leader
articles.summary: The Senate is planning to vote to make Senator Markwayne Mullin the new Homeland Security secretary. He is likely to get the job because many senators support him. In London, police are looking into a fire that burned ambulances belonging to a Jewish group. They think it was a hate crime against Jewish people. No one was hurt, and the police are searching for three people. In Cuba, the electricity keeps going out, making life hard for people. They have trouble keeping food fresh and don't know when the power will come back on. These are three big news stories from around the world.

articles.title: A New Statue for Christopher Columbus Near the White House
articles.summary: President Donald Trump has put a new statue of Christopher Columbus near the White House. This statue is a copy of one that was thrown into a harbor in Baltimore in 2020 during protests. Trump thinks Columbus is a hero because he led a voyage in 1492 that started European exploration in America. But some people are upset because Columbus hurt Native Americans when he arrived. The statue is made of marble and was made by a sculptor in Maryland. A group that owns the statue lent it to the government. Trump wants to honor Columbus, while others celebrate Indigenous Peoples Day instead. This shows different views about history.
